product.title: The Complete Isaac Asimov Foundation Series (Books 1–7) – Includes all Foundation Series Books - {Mass Market Paperback}
product.description: Important Format InformationThis is a mass market paperback set.Books are smaller than standard paperbacks.Ideal for portability, not large-print reading.Explore one of the greatest science fiction sagas ever written with The Complete Isaac Asimov Foundation Series 7-Book Set. This masterfully crafted collection brings together all seven novels of Asimov’s legendary Foundation universe—a groundbreaking narrative that redefined science fiction and inspired generations of readers and creators, including major adaptations for television and film.Set over thousands of years in the future, the series follows visionary mathematician Hari Seldon, who develops a scientific method called psychohistory to predict the fall of the Galactic Empire and guide humanity through the coming dark age. From political intrigue and galactic conflict to philosophical explorations of fate and free will, Asimov's timeless work blends science, strategy, and speculative genius.This mass market paperback edition includes all seven essential books in the reading order recommended by fans and scholars of the series:Included in the Set:Prelude to FoundationJourney to the beginning of the Foundation saga. Set centuries before the original trilogy, this novel introduces Hari Seldon, the brilliant mathematician who arrives on the capital planet Trantor. As he develops his revolutionary theory of psychohistory—a method of predicting the future—he is thrust into political intrigue and danger. A thrilling origin story that sets the philosophical and strategic groundwork for the entire series.Forward the FoundationIn this poignant and powerful continuation of Seldon’s story, we witness his final years as he works to complete the Foundation plan amid growing threats from a crumbling empire. As he loses loved ones and confronts mounting political chaos, Seldon races to preserve his vision for humanity’s survival. A deeply human look at a man struggling to leave a legacy that will guide generations to come.FoundationThe cornerstone of modern science fiction. As the Galactic Empire begins to collapse, Hari Seldon establishes the Foundation, a scientific outpost at the edge of the galaxy designed to preserve knowledge and shorten the coming dark age. Through a series of gripping short stories, we follow the Foundation’s rise, facing external threats, internal politics, and the weight of Seldon’s predictions.Foundation and EmpireThe Foundation’s influence grows, but new dangers emerge. First, the remnants of the Galactic Empire challenge its authority. Then comes The Mule, a powerful mutant whose unpredictable nature disrupts Seldon’s carefully laid plans. With the future at risk, the Foundation must face a threat no one saw coming. A gripping tale of power, strategy

reviewsInfo.customersSay: Customers consider this science fiction series one of the best ever written, praising its readability and value for money. Moreover, they appreciate the writing quality, with one customer noting how it combines elements from the Robot series, and another highlighting its unique perspective on history. Additionally, customers find it makes a wonderful gift, with one mentioning it's perfect for science fiction enthusiasts. However, several customers report missing pages in the books.

reviews.reviewTitle: Its Isaac Asimov's Best Work! Whats not to like! The master of Sci-Fi!
reviews.reviewText: Arrived on time and well packed/protected. I've not read any of these books but I know the general story and am excited to read the real story from the incredible Isaac Asimov himself, rather than get some bloated or modified (by some cornball director who believes THEIR changes to an already perfect, best version, (The authors original vision and any further "original author, authorized updates of said stories. 5 stars I am certain!

reviews.reviewTitle: The books are smaller dimensionally than the one I got at Barnes & Noble
reviews.reviewText: I hadn't read the Foundation series since I was in high school about 50 years ago. I happened to be in B&N saw the "first" Foundation book and picked it up. I decided to get the rest - I didn't realize there were four more books in the series. Like I said, 50 years ago. Ordering them through B&N was a little expensive, so I checked what was available on Amazon. I found this and ordered the entire set even though I would have two "first" Foundation books. My only surprise was that the books only measured 6-3/4" by 4-1/8". The book from the store measured 7-3/4" by 5". The print size seems about the same. The larger books are thinner have fewer pages, but the content seems to be the same. It may seem strange to some, but to me the larger books are nicer to hold than the smaller ones are. It's a personal preference. As for Asimov's writing, I found it less impressive than I remembered it to be (like I said, 50 years). The dialogue seems overly verbose especially in the prequels and sequels to the original trilogy. Although they may share the same kernel of an idea, the books and the Apple TV series are vastly different. I like the TV version better, which is probably why I decided to go back to the books in the first place.
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on October 1, 2024

reviews.reviewText: I originally bought a $4 omnibus edition of the original Foundation trilogy but was sorely disappointed. It was riddled with typos and layout mistakes. I assumed the books weren't written that way, so I ordered this set. I only received these this morning so I can't speak to how well the binding will hold up, but they feel pretty standard so I'm not worried. They were securely packaged in thick well-sealed plastic. A lot of my boxes arrive damaged and, rarely, d but the plastic would've stood up to water and it prevented the books from shifting around and sustaining damage. I won't reveal any plot points but the narrative structure definitely bears some remarks. The short version is I was absolutely floored by the first book. There are a million excellent reasons Foundation is given so many accolades. I've only read the first book in its entirety so far. It's a series of interconnected short stories though it has an axis the whole thing revolves around. There isn't a contiguous narrative per se. I'm a big fan of Brandon Sanderson and his body of work has some interesting parallels in this regard. His series tend to follow more chronologically contiguous arcs but are often interspersed with the sort of one-off character vignettes not unlike Asimov's original short stories. It was disconcerting at first to move through the timeline like that, but his style very clearly draws threads from the very beginning of the book to the end. I devoured the first book in under ten hours, which were some of the most captivating I've had recently. The world Asimov has built is reminiscent of Dune but is much harder science fiction. Not to mention, it's easier and quicker to consume. I'm going backwards to read Prelude to Foundation before I move on to Foundation and Empire. If you, like me, started with Foundation, you won't have any trouble understanding what's going on. After all, Foundation was published first. So far, reading them out of chronological order isn't a problem.
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on June 12, 2019

reviews.reviewText: Great deal for a series of 7. Delivered in perfect condition. Thank you seller!

reviews.reviewTitle: Fast delivery and a happy father.
reviews.reviewText: I bought these books for my Dad as a Father’s Day present. He’d read three of them in the 60’s but wanted to read them again. He didn’t.t know there was now seven books in the series. He thought it was a trilogy. But he is thrilled with the collection. I know he is happily absorbed in those books right now,
